summary.intent: Fix a bug in the chat UI where the 'Compacting...' status element was being incorrectly removed by blindly targeting lastChild instead of tracking the element explicitly per session.
summary.outcome: Rewrote three WebSocket handlers in websocket.js to track the compacting status element explicitly per session, passed biome linting and Rust validation, committed and pushed the fix, resolved the Greptile review thread, and triggered a third Greptile review round.

repo: Biome is used as the linter/formatter; it may reformat multi-line function calls to single-line on check.
Greptile is used for automated code review in iterative rounds triggered after each push.
Rust validation is run after JS changes, suggesting the project has cross-language build/test checks.

code.finding: The compacting status element must be tracked explicitly per session rather than using lastChild removal, to avoid accidentally removing unrelated DOM elements.
chatAddMsg and status message shape were referenced to understand how to correctly insert/remove the compacting status element.

workflow: Biome check diff review before committing is part of the cleanup workflow to catch formatter-only noise.
Greptile review is triggered manually after each push and polled for completion before proceeding.

friction: Required multiple Greptile polling calls to wait for review completion, adding latency to the iteration loop.
open_items: Greptile round 3 was triggered at the end of the session but results were not yet available — review may surface additional comments.
